qa_pairs.question: What are Large Language Models (LLMs)?
qa_pairs.answer: Large Language Models (LLMs) are generative models capable of generating sophisticated natural language text and can be used to power conversational systems.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of resources are available on this page regarding LLMs?
qa_pairs.answer: On this page, you will find resources and tutorials on how to set up and run LLM training or inference workloads on our systems.

qa_pairs.question: What is the most common repository for LLMs at the time of this writing?
qa_pairs.answer: The Hugging Face Hub is the most common repository for LLMs.

qa_pairs.question: Which Python package includes a command line interface for downloading LLMs?
qa_pairs.answer: The `huggingface_hub` Python package contains a command line interface (CLI) which can be used to download models.

qa_pairs.question: How do I download the `Zephyr-7b-beta` model?
qa_pairs.answer: To download the `Zephyr-7b-beta` model, first install `huggingface_hub` in a virtual environment, then on a login node run: `HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 hf download --max-workers=1 HuggingFaceH4/zephyr-7b-beta`.

qa_pairs.question: Why is the `HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET` variable set during model downloads?
qa_pairs.answer: The `HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET` variable is set to avoid using the `hf_xet` package, which currently leads to failures on our systems despite being meant to make downloading artifacts more efficient.

qa_pairs.question: Is the `hf_xet` package recommended for downloading models?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the `hf_xet` package should not be used at this time because it currently leads to failures on our systems.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more options regarding the Hugging Face ecosystem?
qa_pairs.answer: For more options, please see our article on the Hugging Face ecosystem.
